## ICP

> Source: `sources/Marketing & SEO/ICP.md`

# ICP

Disclaimer

The information in this document is a personal opinion on our products and services as coming from the staff members who have limited field experience. It should not be treated as factual unless it matches information provided by founders.

Partnership Manager Perspective

1. Ideal Partner Profile



Это партнёр, который:

— работает в fintech / banking / M&A / licensing / corporate services / fundraising / advisory

— имеет тёплый доступ к владельцам, брокерам, инвесторам, buyers или sellers

— может давать интро по лицензированным активам, а не просто “какой-то интерес”

— понимает, что такое EMI / PI / PSP / bank / fintech asset / regulated business

— готов работать по понятной модели: интро — квалификация — сопровождение — комиссия
— готов работать по понятной модели: интро — квалификация — сопровождение — комиссия

— не исчезает после первого контакта

— умеет быстро подтвердить источник лида и статус отношений с ним



Лучшие типы партнёров для нас:

— M&A advisors / boutique brokers

— consultants по EMI / PI / licensing

— corporate service providers

— legal / compliance firms с fintech-клиентами

— introducers с investor network

— sell-side intermediaries

— buy-side scouts

— fintech ecosystem connectors

— family office / VC / private investor intermediaries, если они реально водят сделки



2. Качественный лид - это тот, где есть:

— конкретная страна / регион

— тип актива или запроса

— buy-side / sell-side / fundraising / strategic acquisition

— краткий фон кейса

— стадия

— срочность
— краткий фон кейса

— стадия

— срочность

— готовность к интодукции

— адекватные ожидания по бюджету и срокам



Наиболее сильные направления:

— покупка / продажа licensed financial institutions

— EMI / PI / PSP / bank opportunities

— fintech M&A

— investment / fundraising around regulated financial businesses

— off-market regulated asset search



Sales Manager Perspective

ICP и продуктовая матрица N5Deal
— off-market regulated asset search



Sales Manager Perspective

ICP и продуктовая матрица N5Deal

1. Общая логика продукта N5DealN5Deal - это M&A платформа, сфокусированная на продаже и покупке готовых финансовых, fintech, crypto и лицензированных активов. В рамках платформы клиенту можно предлагать как готовые компании и лицензии, так и смежные решения под его задачу: подбор актива, выход в новую юрисдикцию, licensing path, builder-направление или упаковку компании к продаже.

Основная логика N5Deal строится не вокруг абстрактного перечня лицензий, а вокруг конкретного запроса клиента. В зависимости от его цели мы подбираем, что именно ему предлагать: готовый asset, EMI, PI, MSB, crypto company, builder-решение или сопровождение продажи.
2. Основные продуктовые направления N5Deal2.1. Marketplace / M&A dealsПродажа и покупка готовых активов: EMI, PI, MSB, crypto companies, fintech companies, investment companies, regulated businesses и других структур в разных юрисдикциях.

2.2. Licensing / IncorporationРешения для клиентов, которым не подходит покупка готового актива и которым нужен путь через получение лицензии или запуск новой структуры.

2.3. Fintech BuilderПостроение fintech-проекта под ключ: структура, лицензирование, AML/KYC, операционная модель, инфраструктура, запуск.

2.4. Sell-side preparation / BoostПодготовка актива к продаже: упаковка, усиление инвестиционной привлекательности, valuation support, due diligence readiness, compliance cleanup.
2.5. Deal SupportПоддержка клиента в процессе сделки: подбор актива, первичная квалификация, структурирование запроса, сопровождение на этапе переговоров и сделки.

3. Основная логика по продуктам и лицензиям3.1. MSBПодходит клиентам, которым нужен более быстрый и доступный вход, чаще всего под США и Канаду. Это решение актуально для money transmission, remittance, crypto-fiat operations и тестового запуска модели.

3.2. PIОптимальный продукт для payment startups, processing, remittance, acquiring, open banking и payment infrastructure. Подходит, когда клиенту нужна лицензированная payment company без более сложной EMI-модели.
3.3. EMIРешение для более масштабных fintech-моделей: digital wallet, multicurrency accounts, stored balances, card issuing, neobank, e-wallet, более сильный valuation и M&A profile.

3.4. Ready-made assetsПодходят клиентам, которым критична скорость выхода на рынок и которые готовы купить уже существующую лицензированную компанию, crypto business, fintech company или другой готовый финансовый актив.

4. ICP и продуктовая матрица: кому что продаём

4.1. ICP 1 - Fintech FounderЭто founder или команда, у которых есть идея, MVP или бизнес-модель, но нет лицензии и инфраструктуры.Основная боль - непонимание, как быстро и легально выйти на рынок, сколько это займёт времени и какой путь выбрать.

Что продаём:

Fintech Builder

Licensing / Incorporation
Что продаём:

Fintech Builder

Licensing / Incorporation

Marketplace, если критична скорость запуска

Что предлагаем:

PI - если речь о payment flow, processing, remittance, open banking

EMI - если нужны wallets, balances, multicurrency, cards

MSB - если фокус на North America или нужен более доступный вход

Ready-made asset - если клиенту нужен быстрый запуск без ожидания лицензирования

4.2. ICP 2 - Crypto Founder / Crypto TeamЭто команда, которая хочет запустить crypto exchange, crypto rails, tokenization, wallet, fiat-crypto bridge или related infrastructure.Основная боль - сложность запуска, регулирование, банковская инфраструктура, комплаенс.

Что продаём:

Fintech Builder

Licensing path

Marketplace с готовыми crypto-related assets

Что предлагаем:
Fintech Builder

Licensing path

Marketplace с готовыми crypto-related assets

Что предлагаем:

MSB - если нужен быстрый вход, особенно под Северную Америку

Crypto-related regulated structure - если проект завязан именно на crypto use case

PI - если упор на payments без полноценной wallet logic

EMI - если нужны wallet, balances, card infrastructure

Ready-made crypto company - если важен быстрый выход на рынок

4.3. ICP 3 - Payment Startup / Payment EntrepreneurЭто проект под acquiring, remittance, merchant services, processing, open banking или payment infrastructure.Основная боль - долгий путь к лицензии и задержка выхода на рынок.

Что продаём:

PI как основной продукт

EMI как расширенное решение

Ready-made asset через marketplace
PI как основной продукт

EMI как расширенное решение

Ready-made asset через marketplace

Builder как сопровождение после покупки или запуска

Что предлагаем:

PI - как основной и наиболее логичный продукт

EMI - если бизнес-модель требует wallet / balances / cards

Ready-made PI / EMI asset - если time-to-market критичен

4.4. ICP 4 - Investor / Fund / M&A BuyerЭто инвестиционный фонд, family office, strategic buyer или частный инвестор, который ищет структурированный финансовый актив.Основная боль - найти понятный актив с внятной логикой, прозрачной структурой и хорошим market entry potential.

Что продаём:

Marketplace

Deal Support / Advisory

Post-acquisition support при необходимости

Что предлагаем:

Готовые EMI, PI, crypto companies, investment firms, regulated entities
Что предлагаем:

Готовые EMI, PI, crypto companies, investment firms, regulated entities

Активы в нужных юрисдикциях

Структуры с потенциалом масштабирования или быстрого выхода на рынок

4.5. ICP 5 - Corporate Expansion / Existing Financial CompanyЭто уже работающая финансовая или fintech-компания, которой нужно выйти в новую юрисдикцию или быстро получить regulatory wrapper.Основная боль - долго лицензироваться заново, при этом нужно ускорить выход на рынок.

Что продаём:

Marketplace

Licensing / Incorporation

Deal Support / Structuring

Что предлагаем:

Готовый PI / EMI / crypto-regulated entity / other licensed structure

New incorporation, если покупать актив невыгодно

Решение под конкретную юрисдикцию и модель масштабирования
Решение под конкретную юрисдикцию и модель масштабирования

4.6. ICP 6 - Seller / Passive Owner / Distressed SellerЭто владелец лицензии или компании, которая не используется, простаивает, требует обслуживания или уже не входит в стратегию собственника.Основная боль - актив не работает в полную силу, требует расходов и при этом не приносит ожидаемой ценности.

Что продаём:

Boost / sell-side preparation

Marketplace listing

Deal structuring / advisory support

Что предлагаем:

Упаковку актива к продаже

Compliance cleanup

Valuation support

Усиление презентации и логики листинга

Вывод актива в structured deal flow
Valuation support

Усиление презентации и логики листинга

Вывод актива в structured deal flow

4.7. ICP 7 - Licensed but Not LaunchedЭто клиент, у которого уже есть лицензия или зарегистрированная структура, но нет команды, операций, банковских партнёров или go-to-market logic.Основная боль - лицензия есть, а полноценного бизнеса на её основе нет.

Что продаём:

Operational support

Builder-light

Business development / partnerships

Marketplace, если клиент уже не хочет запускать актив самостоятельно

Что предлагаем:

AML/KYC setup

Staffing

Banking / payment infrastructure setup

Operational launch support

Growth support

Alternative exit через продажу актива

5. Итоговая логика для насМы продаём не просто лицензии или компании.Мы продаём решение под задачу клиента.
Основные сценарии клиента:

Хочу купить готовый актив

Хочу лицензироваться

Хочу построить fintech-проект с нуля

Хочу выйти в новую юрисдикцию

Хочу продать свой актив

Хочу доупаковать или запустить уже существующую структуру

Основной принцип продажи:

Сначала определяем ICP

Потом понимаем задачу клиента

После этого смотрим на бюджет, юрисдикцию и сроки

И только потом подбираем решение: ready-made asset, EMI, PI, MSB, builder, sell-side prep или сопровождение сделки

## n5deal_icp_strategy

> Source: `sources/Marketing & SEO/n5deal_icp_strategy.md`

# n5deal_icp_strategy

N5Deal — ICP Strategy 

 
 

 
 

 
 Save File
 

✓ File downloaded

 

 

 
N5Deal.com — Internal Strategy Doc

 
# ICP Strategy:Who We're Actually Built For

 
This isn't about who can afford a license. It's about who is trying to solve a financial infrastructure problem — and whether N5Deal is the right tool to solve it.

 

 

 

 00 — Context 
 01 — Framework 
 02 — ICP Segments 
 03 — Key Insight 
 04 — Website 
 05 — Summary 
 

 

 

 
Section 00

 
## What You Are Actually Solving

 
Most fintech platforms sell licenses. N5Deal solves a higher-order problem. The difference in how you frame this determines who shows up at your door — and whether they're worth talking to.

 

 

 
WRONG FRAME

 
We sell licenses
WRONG FRAME

 
We sell licenses

 
This attracts price shoppers, people hunting for the cheapest EMI, and underprepared investors who don't know what they need. These deals take the most time and close the least often.

 

 

 
CORRECT FRAME

 
We solve financial infrastructure decisions

 
This attracts operators who understand complexity, founders who need structure, and investors who want to reduce execution risk. These deals close faster, at higher value, with less noise.

 

 

 

 
 The practical consequence of this reframe: ICP is not about "who can afford a license." It's about "who is trying to solve a financial infrastructure problem and is ready to act." Budget and license type are outputs of the conversation — not inputs that define the segment.
Section 01

 
## Core Segmentation Logic

 
Every prospect maps to three independent dimensions. These three layers, read together, tell you more about a client's fit and deal potential than any budget figure ever will.

 

 

 
LAYER 01

 
Decision Maturity

 
How clearly has the client articulated what they actually need? This determines how much education is required before a deal can happen.

 

 Abstract — "I want fintech" 
 Semi-structured — "Something like EMI in EU" 
 Defined — Jurisdiction, timeline, budget clear 
 

 

 

 
LAYER 02

 
Action Type

 
What is the client actually trying to do? This determines which product is the right fit for this stage of the relationship.
Buy — acquire existing licensed entity 
 Incorporate — build from scratch 
 Hybrid — buy + restructure / build around 
 

 

 

 
LAYER 03

 
Execution Readiness

 
Can this prospect actually close? Execution readiness is the real filter — it separates genuinely qualified leads from explorers who consume time without converting.

 

 Capital available and accessible 
 Team or advisors already in place 
 Timeline is real, not hypothetical 
 

 

 

 

 
 Critical distinction: Budget is an attribute, not a segmentation criterion. A $500K deal from a defined, execution-ready operator is worth more than a $5M inquiry from an abstract, unstructured investor. License type is an output — what the client ends up needing — not the input that defines who they are.

 

 

 

 

 
Section 02
Section 02

 
## The 5 ICP Segments

 
Click each segment to expand the full profile. Real-world companies are shown as examples of what these prospects look like in practice.

 

 
 

 

 

 Core Revenue 
 
Advanced Buyer / Operator

 
Has done this before. Knows exactly what they want. Optimises for fit, not price.

 

 
−

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are

 
Founders who have exited or scaled fintech companies before. Senior operators — Head of Payments, CFO, COO — who have spent years inside financial infrastructure. Experienced investors who have backed or built regulated entities and understand what they're buying.
These are not explorers. They arrive with constraints already defined: jurisdiction, timeline, regulatory requirements, often a specific license type. They are evaluating you as a counterparty, not asking you to educate them.

 

 

 
How They Think

 
They don't ask "what can I afford?" They ask: what structure solves my problem fastest, where is the regulatory friction lowest, what gives me control over flows. They will walk away from a mis-fit deal faster than any other segment.

 
Entry Moment & Deal Size

 
Arrives with a fully formed request. Deal size ranges from sub-$1M MSBs to $10M EMI/PI transactions — but size is irrelevant as a filter. A $700K MSB deal from this segment closes in weeks. A $3M inquiry from an abstract investor drags for months.
Real-World Equivalent Prospects

 

 
Airwallex early stage — operators who built Alipay rails, knew exactly what EMI structure they needed in EU 

 
Paysend — cross-border payments operators with defined infrastructure requirements from day one 

 
Ex-Wise / Revolut operators — launching second ventures with full knowledge of what licensed entity to acquire 

 
Rapyd — fintech infrastructure founders with prior payment company experience who knew the Buy path 

 

 

 

 
Why N5Deal

 
Their core pain is wasted time — non-serious sellers, inaccurate listings, low-quality deal flow. N5Deal's value is filtering the noise and compressing the time between intent and execution. You are their shortcut to a vetted pipeline.
High Volume 
 
Structured Fintech Builder

 
Building a real product. Knows they need infrastructure. Doesn't know which path to take yet.

 

 
+

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are

 
Startup founders entering fintech, SaaS founders adding a financial layer to an existing product, and operators building embedded finance. The product exists or is being built. The infrastructure decision — what to build, where to incorporate, whether to buy or license — is unresolved.

 
These clients are smart, move fast, and are vulnerable to making expensive structural mistakes early. They are researching, comparing options, and suffering from the chaos of conflicting information online.

 

 

 
How They Think
How They Think

 
They know they need "financial infrastructure" but the path is unclear. Buy vs. build. Which jurisdiction — Lithuania, UK, UAE, Singapore? What license is sufficient for their use case? They fear making the wrong long-term decision and getting locked into a structure that limits them later.

 
Action Type

 
 
Primarily Incorporate or Hybrid

 
Sometimes Buy when speed matters most

 
Deal size varies widely — cheap license ≠ simple case

 
 

 

 

 
Real-World Equivalent Prospects

 

 
Shopify (early Payments phase) — SaaS adding embedded payments, needed clear infrastructure structure in multiple markets 

 
Deel — HR platform that needed EMI/PI licenses to move payroll money globally without third-party dependency
Brex — startup building corporate cards, needed to navigate US/EU license decisions from scratch 

 
Wagestream — embedded finance product that had to figure out whether to incorporate or license an existing EMI 

 

 

 

 
Why N5Deal

 
This segment needs someone to turn chaos into a framework: what are the actual options, what are the real trade-offs, what is the right structure for this specific product. N5Deal's Fintech Builder service converts confusion into a concrete roadmap — then closes the deal once clarity is established.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 High Potential / High Risk 
 
Capital-Backed, Low-Context Investor

 
Has money. Thinks fintech is attractive. Has no clear picture of what they actually need.

 

 
+

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are
+

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are

 
Private investors, family capital, non-fintech founders post-exit. They are attracted to financial infrastructure as an asset class or diversification play — not because they have a specific product vision. They often arrive with a large number and a vague brief: "I want to get into fintech."

 
This segment is dangerous because budget is a misleading signal. They can have $5M available and still be the worst-fit client. Conversely, a well-structured investor in this category can become one of the most valuable long-term relationships.

 

 

 
How They Think
How They Think

 
Fintech feels attractive. They've watched Revolut, Wise, and Stripe from the outside and want exposure. But there's no underlying product logic — they are often jumping between "buy a license," "build something," and "find a partner" within the same conversation.

 
The Core Risk

 
 
Fear and lack of trust block action

 
Cannot evaluate options without help

 
Prone to making expensive structural errors

 
Long sales cycles unless structured early

 
 

 

 

 
Real-World Equivalent Prospect Types

 

 
Real estate family offices — post-property boom capital looking for new asset classes in fintech infrastructure 

 
E-commerce founder exits — founder sold a Shopify store empire, now exploring "own payments infrastructure"
UHNW angel investors — backing fintech startups but now want direct exposure via a licensed entity 

 
Post-exit SaaS founders — sold their B2B tool, exploring whether a payment license makes sense for the next chapter 

 

 

 

 
Why N5Deal

 
The job here is to convert vague interest into a structured decision — and protect the client from buying the wrong thing. The Fintech Builder framework is the entry point. N5Deal closes the deal once clarity is established. Without that structuring step, these clients either don't convert or buy incorrectly and become unhappy.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 High Urgency 
 
High-Risk Operators

 
Crypto, gambling, high-risk merchants. Payment stability is existential — not strategic.

 

 
+

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are
+

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are

 
Crypto exchanges, gambling and betting operators, high-risk merchants who have been debanked, rate-limited, or cut off by payment processors. These are not companies exploring options — they have an operational problem that is actively costing them revenue right now.

 
Their willingness to act is high. What they need is access to structures that most mainstream brokers don't know exist or won't touch.

 

 

 
How They Think

 
"We need payments to survive." The urgency is real, the budget constraint is regulatory, not financial. They aren't haggling on price — they're asking whether you can actually solve their specific compliance and structural problem in their specific jurisdiction.

 
Why This Segment Is Hard
Why This Segment Is Hard

 
 
Limited options globally — most brokers won't touch them

 
Regulatory pressure complicates every structure

 
High urgency creates fast decisions — and fast mistakes

 
 

 

 

 
Real-World Equivalent Prospects

 

 
Kraken (pre-license era) — crypto exchange needing fiat on/off rails and a stable licensed entity to avoid debanking 

 
Nexo — crypto lender needing regulated infrastructure across multiple jurisdictions to access banking 

 
Bet365 international expansion — gambling operator needing payment control in new markets 

 
Bitpanda — European crypto platform that acquired an EMI specifically to control payment flows independently 

 

 

 

 
Why N5Deal
Why N5Deal

 
N5Deal's value for this segment is unique access. Most platforms don't list structures suitable for high-risk operators — they self-select out. N5Deal's ability to understand the risk landscape and surface hard-to-find structures is the entire proposition for this segment.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 Long-Cycle / High-Value 
 
Institutional Buyers

 
PE funds, corporates, family offices with a thesis. Long cycle, high value, needs pre-screening.

 

 
+

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are
+

 

 

 

 

 
Who They Are

 
Large fintech or financial companies internalising payments infrastructure, PE funds acquiring licensed entities for portfolio value, and family offices or holding companies building financial infrastructure as a long-term strategic asset. The decision framework is thesis-driven — they've already done the analysis and are now in execution mode.

 

 

 
How They Think

 
Structured evaluation, internal alignment required, due diligence is thorough. These clients don't need education — they need efficiency. The barrier isn't knowledge or capital; it's internal process, approval chains, and the quality of deal flow surfaced to their team.

 
Deal Characteristics

 
 
Medium to large deal size, secondary to strategic fit
Deal Characteristics

 
 
Medium to large deal size, secondary to strategic fit

 
Long sales cycle — 6 to 18 months typical

 
Very high close rate once internal alignment achieved

 
 

 

 

 
Real-World Equivalent Prospects

 

 
Klarna — acquired a German bank license rather than applying, to accelerate EU expansion timeline 

 
Stripe Treasury — built embedded banking by acquiring licensed infrastructure rather than going direct 

 
Paysafe — PE-backed fintech that has made multiple licensed entity acquisitions to build infrastructure 

 
Checkout.com — acquired licensed PSPs in new markets rather than applying from scratch 

 

 

 

 
Why N5Deal
Why N5Deal

 
Pre-screening is the product for this segment. Their internal teams don't have time to evaluate 200 listings. N5Deal's value is that by the time a deal reaches their desk, the noise has been removed. Every hour saved in their due diligence process is direct value delivered.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Section 03

 
## The Most Important Strategic Insight

 

 
"The same license is the right answer for five completely different clients — and the wrong answer for five others who think they need it."
MSB, EMI, PI — these are outputs of the conversation, not filters on the intake. An MSB under $1M is a perfect tool for an experienced operator who knows exactly what they're doing with it. The same license, sold to a capital-backed investor with no product clarity, will sit unused and generate a complaint six months later.

 

 

 

 
IMPLICATION 01

 
Stop selling licenses. Start diagnosing problems.

 
The intake conversation should establish action type and decision maturity before any product is mentioned. The license recommendation is the conclusion — not the opening line.

 

 

 
IMPLICATION 02

 
Budget as a filter will destroy your deal quality.
IMPLICATION 02

 
Budget as a filter will destroy your deal quality.

 
Filtering on budget early surfaces the lowest-quality prospects. A $500K operator who knows what they need is worth more than a $3M investor who doesn't. Qualify on decision maturity and execution readiness instead.

 

 

 
IMPLICATION 03

 
The platform serves the same market at different moments.

 
Fintech Builder is the decision layer — it structures the client and moves them to clarity. N5Deal M&A is the execution layer — it closes the deal once the brief is defined. They are sequential, not separate.

 

 

 

 

 

 
Section 04

 
## How to Deliver This on n5deal.com
Section 04

 
## How to Deliver This on n5deal.com

 
Based on direct analysis of n5deal.com, here are the structural gaps and the recommended changes to reflect this ICP strategy in the product itself.

 

 
Current Website — What It Gets Right and What It Doesn't

 
The site is clean and well-built. The homepage hero ("M&A Platform for the Financial Sector — Buy or Sell Financial Business & License Assets") is accurate but transactional. It addresses people who already know they want to buy or sell — skipping entirely the large segment who hasn't decided yet.

 
 ✕ First navigation fork is "Seller / Buyer" — forces a transaction label before the problem is understood
✕ No onboarding for the Capital-Backed Investor or Structured Builder who doesn't know which category they are 

 
 ✕ License types (Crypto, PSP, EMI, PI, Bank) shown as primary filter — but licenses are outputs, not inputs 

 
 ✕ Fintech Builder is buried in navigation — it's the highest-value differentiator and should be front and centre 

 

 
 ✓ Strong credibility signals — 300+ deals, 36+ countries, 1,000+ qualified buyers — should be higher on the page 

 
 ✓ AI Matching is a real differentiator that could be surfaced much earlier in the flow 

 
 ✓ Fintech Builder exists as a product — it just needs to be repositioned as the decision-layer entry point 

 

 
Recommended User Journey — ICP-Driven Flow
Recommended User Journey — ICP-Driven Flow

 
Instead of "are you a buyer or seller?", the site should ask "what are you trying to solve?" — then route the user to the right product path. This single change aligns intake with the actual ICP logic.

 

 

 
1

 

 
#### What are you trying to do?

 
The first question is about the problem, not the transaction. This routes by action type — which maps directly to the ICP segmentation above.

 

 Launch a fintech product 
 Control my payment infrastructure 
 Enter a new market 
 Acquire a regulated business 
 Build around a license 
 

 

 

 

 
2

 

 
#### How clear is your path?
2

 

 
#### How clear is your path?

 
This surfaces decision maturity — which determines whether the client needs Fintech Builder (decision layer) or can go straight to M&A deal flow (execution layer).

 

 I'm exploring options 
 I'm comparing specific paths 
 I'm ready to execute 
 

 

 

 

 
3

 

 
#### Output: route to the right product

 
Based on answers 1 + 2, the site routes the user to the correct path. All routes end in a concrete next step — never a dead end.

 

 → Buy via N5Deal M&A 
 → Incorporate via Fintech Builder 
 → Hybrid path 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
CONTENT STRATEGY

 
Map content to segments, not to license types
CONTENT STRATEGY

 
Map content to segments, not to license types

 
Current blog covers market news. High-leverage alternative: segment-specific content. "How ex-operators structure their second fintech" (Segment 1). "Buy vs. build: the real trade-offs in 2026" (Segment 2 + 3). "Why crypto operators are acquiring EMIs instead of applying" (Segment 4). Each piece attracts the right ICP and pre-qualifies them before first contact.

 

 

 
HEADLINE UPGRADE

 
Lead with the decision, not the transaction

 
The homepage headline opportunity: "We help you make the right financial infrastructure decision — and execute it." This captures both Fintech Builder and N5Deal M&A, positions at a higher level than any competitor, and is true to how the best deals actually work.
Section 05

 
## ICP Summary Table

 
A single-view reference of all five segments. Use this as a qualification checklist when evaluating incoming leads.

 

 
 
 

 Segment 
 Decision Maturity 
 Action Type 
 Deal Size Logic 
 Core Problem 
 N5Deal Product 
 

 
 
 

 Advanced Buyer / Operator 
 High 
 Buy Hybrid 
 Irrelevant — fit-driven 
 Speed to market + infrastructure control 
 M&A Deal Flow — execution layer 
 

 

 Structured Fintech Builder 
 Medium 
 Incorporate Hybrid 
 Highly variable — complexity ≠ price 
 Correct architecture + avoiding wrong setup 
 Fintech Builder → M&A 
 

 

 Capital-Backed Investor 
 Low–Medium 
 Buy Incorporate 
 Misleading — budget ≠ readiness 
 Risk reduction + structured entry 
 Fintech Builder → qualify → M&A
High-Risk Operator 
 High 
 Buy 
 Constrained by regulation, not capital 
 Payment survival + independence 
 M&A Deal Flow — rare structures 
 

 

 Institutional Buyer 
 High 
 Buy 
 Medium–large, secondary to strategic fit 
 Efficiency + pre-screened deal flow 
 M&A Deal Flow — curation + speed 
 

 
 
 

 

 
Qualification Priority Order
Qualification Priority Order

 
For sales prioritisation: Segment 1 and Segment 4 have the highest readiness and close fastest — they should receive immediate direct response. Segment 5 has the highest deal value but longest cycle — assign to a dedicated relationship track. Segment 2 is the highest volume and best long-term pipeline — Fintech Builder should be the primary entry point. Segment 3 requires structured qualification before resource investment; without it, they consume disproportionate time relative to conversion rate.

 

 

 

 
## The Positioning That Ties It Together

 
"We don't sell licenses. We help you make the right financial infrastructure decision — and execute it."

# Copy of Інформація для заповнення картки есету на платформі

Загальна інформація



Kazakhstan – EMI (Main License) (EUR 1,500,000)



Country: Kazakhstan



Type of License: MAIN: EMI (Electronic Money Institution) – Central Bank of Kazakhstan license



Type of Business: E-money / payments platform (merchant processing readiness)



Business Status: Not Active (no significant operational activity; only live merchant tests)



Employee Count: Operational team + CEO in place (headcount N/A)



Year of License Issue: N/A



Last Year Revenue/Expenses: N/A



Asking Price (incl. success fee / range): EUR 1,500,000 + 10% (deal range €1M–€3M) = EUR 1,650,000



Short Business Description (≤50): KZ EMI + PCI DSS; Visa high-risk cert in progress
Short Business Description (≤50): KZ EMI + PCI DSS; Visa high-risk cert in progress



Benefits: Acquiring, Payment Gateway, Software, Staff, Support, Security, Multi-Currency

Benefits



From the list: Acquiring, Payment Gateway, Software, Staff, Support, Security, Multi-Currency



Additional:



Built infrastructure and completed live merchant tests



Visa high-risk processing certification in final stage



Multiple active local bank accounts; team/CEO in place



Regulator: Central Bank of Kazakhstan



Asking Price (formula): EUR 1,500,000 + 10% = EUR 1,650,000
Asking Price (formula): EUR 1,500,000 + 10% = EUR 1,650,000





В 4й Крок

Додаткова інформація, яка підтягується в офери + видно у вкладці Details

Те що жовтим - НЕ пишеться - тільки текст під ним





УВАЖНО - 5й крок

В 5й крок на платформі, в який можна потрапити через Edit Asset

Може додаватися наша внутрішня інформація, яку буде видно нам як адміністраторам.

# Copy of Приклад для заповнення на DealStream

Nigeria – Licensed Payment Gateway (PSSP) for Sale

Price: USD 950,000

Licensed Payment Solution Service Provider (PSSP) regulated by the Central Bank of Nigeria, offering an operational payment gateway platform with existing merchant integrations and transaction processing infrastructure.

The license enables the company to provide payment gateway services, payment processing, merchant integrations, transaction switching (non-clearing), and value-added payment services, positioning the platform within the Nigerian digital payments ecosystem.

Infrastructure

Proprietary in-house developed payment gateway

API integrations supporting C2B, B2C and B2B payments

Merchant payment processing infrastructure
API integrations supporting C2B, B2C and B2B payments

Merchant payment processing infrastructure

CRM and financial management systems implemented

Banking

Operational banking relationships established with major Nigerian banks:

Guaranty Trust Bank (GTBank)

Zenith Bank

Operations

Licensed and operational fintech infrastructure

4 active merchants

Transaction processing already established

Payment APIs available for integrations

Local presence includes a Managing Director, independent non-executive director, company secretary and compliance oversight, with a physical office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.The operational team is willing to remain post-transaction.

Financial Snapshot

Average monthly revenue: NGN 777,940

Transaction volume (last 6 months):
Financial Snapshot

Average monthly revenue: NGN 777,940

Transaction volume (last 6 months):

Payments in: NGN 857,629,775

Payments out: NGN 481,346,398

Average monthly operating expenses: NGN 7,982,532

Paid-up share capital: NGN 100,000,000

Investment Highlights

Central Bank of Nigeria licensed payment institution

Entry point into one of Africa’s fastest-growing fintech markets

Existing payment gateway infrastructure and banking rails

Active transaction processing and merchant integrations

Platform positioned for scaling digital payments and merchant services

Transaction: Shareholders are open to a 100% sale of the company.



















































Rwanda – Licensed Payment Institution for Sale
Rwanda – Licensed Payment Institution for Sale

Licensed Payment Institution in Rwanda holding a Payment Services License (Category A – Payment Aggregator Service Provider) issued by the National Bank of Rwanda (BNR). The license allows the company to operate merchant payment aggregation and payment gateway services within the Rwandan payment ecosystem.

The company has established regulatory approval, banking infrastructure and payment technology required to support digital payment services in the region.



License & Regulatory

Jurisdiction: RwandaRegulator: National Bank of Rwanda (BNR)

License Type: Payment Services License – Category A (Payment Aggregator Service Provider)

Permitted activities include:
Permitted activities include:

Merchant payment aggregation

Merchant acquiring and transaction processing

Payment transaction execution as part of aggregation

Settlement and disbursement services

Operation of segregated escrow / nodal accounts

Payment gateway and payment interface services

Refund and reversal processing

Ancillary payment support services

These permissions enable the company to operate as a payment aggregator, merchant acquiring platform and payment gateway provider within Rwanda.

Tanzania – Licensed Payment Institution for Sale

The license enables the company to provide electronic payment services including merchant acquiring, payment gateway services and funds transfers within the Tanzanian financial ecosystem.

The company has established the regulatory structure, banking relationships and technology infrastructure required to support digital payment services in the East African market.

Uganda – Licensed Payment Institution for Sale

Licensed Payment Institution in Uganda operating as a Payment Services Provider (PSP) / Payment System Operator (PSO) within the Ugandan fintech ecosystem. The company currently operates under regulatory authorization, with the full license expected to be formally issued by the Bank of Uganda before the end of Q1 2026.

The company has established payment infrastructure, banking relationships and merchant integrations supporting digital payment services across the Ugandan market.



License & Regulatory

Jurisdiction: UgandaRegulator: Bank of Uganda (BOU) and National Information Technology Authority – Uganda (NITA-U)
License Type: Payment Services Provider (PSP) / Payment System Operator (PSO)

Permitted activities include:

Integration with banks, card schemes and payment partners for electronic retail transactions

Processing payment instructions for merchants and customers

Routing funds to merchant wallets or bank accounts

Transaction reconciliation and settlement to merchant accounts

Online payment interfaces and merchant onboarding

Payment analytics and reporting services

Compliance with AML/KYC and safeguarding requirements under Bank of Uganda regulations

These permissions allow the company to operate as a payment gateway, merchant aggregator and payment processing platform in Uganda.



Technology & Infrastructure:

Proprietary in-house developed payment gateway platform
